# Probability Disclosure: KR vs JP (Comparative Matrix)

## Scope and As-of Date
- Jurisdictions: KR, JP
- As-of date: 2026-05-07
- Assumptions: cross-border publisher shipping randomized paid items in both markets
- Exclusions: PRC, EU, US

## Comparative Matrix

| Axis | KR | JP | Divergence Note |
|------|----|----|-----------------|
| Regulatory Scope | Statutory: operators of online games offering randomized paid items [src_001] | Self-regulatory: CESA member publishers [src_002] | KR is binding by statute; JP binding by industry membership only |
| Obligated Parties | Game publisher [src_001] | CESA member publisher [src_002] | JP non-members fall outside the self-regulatory regime |
| Key Obligations | Disclose probabilities at point of purchase [src_001] | Display probability information per CESA Section 3 [src_002] | KR statutory specificity higher; JP framework self-policed |
| Exemptions and Safe Harbors | None on the face of the statute [src_001] | Free-to-play with no monetization gating [src_002] | JP carve-out absent in KR |
| Sanctions and Penalties | Administrative fine plus possible criminal exposure [src_001] | Loss of CESA endorsement; consumer-protection enforcement possible [src_002] | KR sanctions are statutory; JP rely on consumer-protection law |
| Competent Authority | Game Rating and Administration Committee [src_001] | CESA plus Consumer Affairs Agency [src_002] | KR has a specialized regulator |
| Effective Date and Transition | In force from 2024-03-22 [src_001] | N/A (industry guideline) [src_002] | KR statutory effective date controls |
| Extraterritorial Reach | Foreign operators targeting KR residents [src_001] | Membership-based, no extraterritorial trigger [src_002] | KR catches foreign operators directly |
| Cross-Border Mechanisms | Domestic agent designation may apply [src_001] | None [src_002] | KR imposes a localization touchpoint |
| Pending Reforms | MoCST notice on disclosure depth pending [src_001] | None identified [src_002] | KR rule is still tightening |

## Divergence Commentary
- Material difference: KR is binding by statute; JP is binding only on association members [src_001] [src_002].
- Strictest standard: KR for scope, sanctions, and effective-date discipline.
- Convergence trends: both jurisdictions converge on point-of-purchase disclosure as the operational baseline.

## Counter-Analysis
Per `knowledge/output-modes/counter-analysis-checklist.md`. JP enforcement
intensity could rise if Consumer Affairs Agency formalizes the CESA
standard. Risk level Medium for foreign publishers relying solely on
membership compliance.

## Practical Implications
### Compliance Strategy
- Baseline: KR statutory disclosure depth
- Multi-jurisdiction action: a single disclosure UI satisfying KR also satisfies JP

### Risk Hotspots
- Highest risk: launching in KR without statutory-grade disclosure depth
- Active enforcement: KR; JP enforcement is currently soft

### Monitoring Points
- KR MoCST disclosure-depth notice
- JP Consumer Affairs Agency posture toward CESA standard

## Annotated Bibliography
- Primary: [src_001] Korean Game Industry Promotion Act probability disclosure provisions, Article 33-2
- Secondary: [src_002] Japan CESA Self-Regulation Probability Display Guidelines, Sections 1-4

## Verification Guide
- KR statutory disclosure -> [src_001] Article 33-2(1)-(3)
- JP self-regulatory disclosure -> [src_002] Section 3
